steps.title: Anesthetize the mouse
steps.instruction: Anesthetize the mouse.

steps.title: Immerse the delipidated sample in 10...
steps.instruction: Immerse the delipidated sample in 10 mL of PBS containing 5% MeOH in the 15 mL standing tube and incubate it for Overnight at 37 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ).

steps.title: Immerse the fixed sample in 10 mL of 0
steps.instruction: Immerse the fixed sample in 10 mL of 0.5× CUBIC-L (1:1 dilution with water) in the 30 mL tube and incubate it Overnight at 37 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ).

steps.title: During the sample wash, prepare up...
steps.instruction: During the sample wash, prepare up to 10 mL of fixative solution by diluting formalin (FA) solution to 1% in HV wash buffer and cool it On ice . The saturated formalin solution contains 35 to 38% of formalin. For example, when using a 37% formalin solution, dilute it with HV wash buffer at a ratio of 1:36.

steps.title: Transcardially perfuse 10 mL (4 mL/min)...
steps.instruction: Transcardially perfuse 10 mL (4 mL/min) of ice-cold PBS containing 10 U/mL of Heparin.

steps.title: Transcardially perfuse 20 mL (6 mL/min)...
steps.instruction: Transcardially perfuse 20 mL (6 mL/min) of ice-cold 4% (w/v) PFA.

steps.title: Dissect the organs
steps.instruction: Dissect the organs.

steps.title: Wash the sample in PBS containing 0
steps.instruction: Wash the sample in PBS containing 0.05% NaN 3 for 01:00:00 × 3 times at Room temperature with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ).

steps.title: Post-fix the dissected organs in 4%...
steps.instruction: Post-fix the dissected organs in 4% (w/v) PFA in PBS (~ 10 mL /whole organ) for Overnight at 4 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ).

steps.title: Wash the sample with 20 mL of PBS containing 0
steps.instruction: Wash the sample with 20 mL of PBS containing 0.05% NaN 3 for 02:00:00 × 3 times (or 02:00:00 ×1, Overnight ×1, 02:00:00 ×1) at 37 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ). After each use, the tubes should be washed or replaced in order to remove Triton X-100 thoroughly. The delipidated sample can be stored in PBS containing 0.05% NaN 3 at 4 °C for at least several months.

steps.title: Replace with 10 mL to 15...
steps.instruction: Replace with 10 mL to 15 mL of 1× CUBIC-L in the 30 mL tube and delipidate for 72:00:00 to 120:00:00 (3 to 5 days) at 37 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ). Replace CUBIC-L every 48:00:00 to 72:00:00 (2 to 3 days) if the duration of treatment exceeds 72:00:00 (3 days).

steps.title: Replace the HV wash buffer with...
steps.instruction: Replace the HV wash buffer with the new one and continue washing the sample two more times ( 02:00:00 ×2).

steps.title: Replace the HV wash buffer with...
steps.instruction: Replace the HV wash buffer with a new one and continue washing the sample gently at 4 °C with light shielding, while shaking at 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, , for 02:00:00 .

steps.title: Immerse the sample in 10 mL...
steps.instruction: Immerse the sample in 10 mL of pre-cooled HV wash buffer in the 15 mL standing tube. Gently invert the tube several times to separate the wrapping reagent remaining on the sample surface.

steps.title: Remove the residual buffer and the...
steps.instruction: Remove the residual buffer and the HV-LLPS reagent as in step 14.

steps.title: Recover the sample from the staining...
steps.instruction: Recover the sample from the staining pot with a metal spoon. Discard the staining solution and the HV-LLPS reagent.

steps.title: To stabilize the 2nd Fab signals,...
steps.instruction: To stabilize the 2nd Fab signals, further incubate the staining chamber for 24:00:00 at 4 °C with slow rotation (< 1 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition.

steps.title: Close the lid tightly and incubate...
steps.instruction: Close the lid tightly and incubate the chamber for at least 168:00:00 (1 week) at Room temperature with slow rotation (< 1 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition.

steps.title: Slowly pour the HV-LLPS reagent into...
steps.instruction: Slowly pour the HV-LLPS reagent into the staining pot until the pot is filled. Remove the bubbles. HV-LLPS prevents samples from drying out during staining by covering the entire sample and the staining solution, which contains condensed staining probes.

steps.title: Exchange the buffer to 10 mL...
steps.instruction: Exchange the buffer to 10 mL of HV staining buffer (10 mM HEPES [pH 7.5], 10% [w/v] Triton X-100, 500 mM NaCl, and 0.5% [w/v] casein) containing HV-Additive suitable for your primary Ab and incubate it for 01:30:00 at 37 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ).

steps.title: Collect the sample with a metal...
steps.instruction: Collect the sample with a metal spoon and put it into the staining pot. A portion of the sample should be attached gently on soft paper towel to absorb residual buffer.

steps.title: Transfer the entire 3D staining solution...
steps.instruction: Transfer the entire 3D staining solution into the 3D tissue staining pot ( CUBICStars, CSSR005 ). This 3D tissue staining pot for CUBIC-HV2.0 can be used repeatedly. After the usage, wash the pot with neutral detergent and distilled water.

steps.title: During the step 11, prepare primary...
steps.instruction: During the step 11, prepare primary Ab + secondary Fab fragment mix as follows: Calculate the required volume (X, Y) of primary Ab and secondary Fab fragment. Example of calculation is shown bel %s %s ow. %s %s Prepare the following 3D staining solution (typically 50 to 100 µL per whole mouse brain). Example of preparation is shown below. A B 1 Reagent Volume 2 2× HV staining buffer containing HV-Additive 50 µL 3 Primary Ab X µL 4 Secondary Fab Y µL 5 Nuclear stain* Z µL 6 Distilled water 50-(X+Y+Z) µL 7 Total 100 µL The total volume of the staining solution should be determined based on the quantity and final concentration of primary Ab. For instance, in the case of using 5 µg of primary Ab at a concentration of 100 µg/mL, a volume of 50 µL should be prepared for the 3D staining solution (5 µg of primary Ab in 50 µL = 100 µg/mL). Generally, 50-100 µg/mL of antibody concentration prepared in 50-350 µL of the staining solution is recommended. The type of HV-additives should be determined for each primary antibody (please check "Before start" section). Generally, one or two HV-Additive reagent are used. *Recommended amount of nuclear stain BOBO-1 Iodide: 4 µL/whole mouse brain SYTOX Green Nucleic Acid Stain: 1.6 µL/whole mouse brain RedDot2 Far-Red Nuclear Stain: 4 µL/whole mouse brain Add the entire volume to the 500 µL Protein LoBind tube and incubate the mix for 01:30:00 at 37 °C .

steps.title: Incubate the sample in 15 mL of 0
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample in 15 mL of 0.5× CUBIC-R+ (1:1 diluted with distilled water) in a 30 mL tube for 24:00:00 at > 25 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition.

steps.title: Remove the residual diluted CUBIC-R+ as...
steps.instruction: Remove the residual diluted CUBIC-R+ as in section 3-step 5 and incubate the sample in 15 mL (or 30 mL if the tissue will be embedded in agarose gel) of 1× CUBIC-R+ for 48:00:00 (2 days) at > 25 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition. Carryover of residual diluted CUBIC-R+ results in a poor clearing result due to a decrease in the final refractive index. CUBIC-R+ crystallizes easily in winter. Ensure that sample in the reagent are handled and incubated at 25°C or higher. If required, CUBIC-R+-cleared samples can be embedded in 2% agarose/CUBIC-R+. For more details, please refer to our protocol paper and our resource website.

steps.title: Use the sample for microscopic observation (gel embedding if necessary)
steps.instruction: Use the sample for microscopic observation (gel embedding if necessary)

steps.title: Wash the sample in 30 mL...
steps.instruction: Wash the sample in 30 mL of PBS in the 50 mL tube for 02:00:00 at Room temperature with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition.

steps.title: To accelerate the fixation reaction, further...
steps.instruction: To accelerate the fixation reaction, further incubate the sample for 01:00:00 at 37 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition. Reducing the FA reaction time in steps 25 and 26 could result in the attenuation of antibody signals.

steps.title: Fix the sample in the 1%...
steps.instruction: Fix the sample in the 1% FA for 24:00:00 at 4 °C with gentle shaking ( 40 rpm, °C, to 50 rpm, °C, ) under a light-shielded condition.

steps.title: One hour before moving to the...
steps.instruction: One hour before moving to the step 19, prepare HV wash buffer (10 mM HEPES [pH 7.5], 10% [w/v] Triton X-100, 500 mM NaCl) and cool it On ice .
